definitions.definition: Of or akin to; related; like

A male having at least one parent in common with another (see half-brother, stepbrother).
A male fellow member of a religious community, church, trades union etc.
definitions.example: I would like to thank the brother who just spoke.

definitions.definition: A form of address to a man.
definitions.example: Listen, brother, I don't know what you want, but I'm not interested.

definitions.definition: A black male.
Somebody, usually male, connected by a common cause or situation.
Someone who is a peer, whether male or female.

Crew and consoles remain on the lower surrounding level, emphasizing hierarchy and elevation.

Environment: Icy, lunar terrain, with a sprawling gantry bridge suspended over a steaming, methane-rich chasm, bathed in Jupiter's distant auroras.
Activities: A lone maintenance worker, suited up, performs repairs on the gantry's support struts, while below, a team of researchers study unique, glowing bio-luminescent flora thriving near the chasm's edge, ignoring the distant rumble of a Europa quake.

hewn.definition: To chop away at; to whittle down; to mow down.

definitions.definition: To chop away at; to whittle down; to mow down.
To shape; to form.
definitions.example: One of the most widely used typefaces in the world was hewn by the English printer and typographer John Baskerville.

definitions.definition: To act according to, to conform to; usually construed with to.
Made or crafted by cutting, whittling down.
Having been cut or mown down.
